decision_taken: MaterialTypeHandle is the taxonomy term NAME (string): auto_create:TRUE means referencing a name IS creating the term, so a separate term-entity event would model storage, not domain
reversal_condition: a fixture distinguishing two terms with one name, or term metadata beyond the name

topic: material metadata on the shared quantity record
decision_taken: quantityType/materialTypes ride the SHARED QuantityRecord instead of a new event kind, so input adds zero kinds and parallelizes on the shared taxonomy alone; default_quantity_type: material is applied as a default, never a restriction
reversal_condition: another feature needs material quantities with different semantics (promote a shape decision)

decision_taken: listInputLogs admits a log once when at least one quantity carries the requested term (the IN-subquery shape); no glossary probe can exercise it, so it is test-covered but oracle-unverifiable — flagged in scope.md as a DECIDE punt
reversal_condition: a material-type assertion term lands in the glossary

decision_taken: purchase_date is inert extras metadata; it never participates in effective-time ordering (scope classification), asserted by test
reversal_condition: an observed fixture where purchase_date moves a views ordering the port must honor

decision_taken: the contract's recordMaterialInput takes no asset argument, so those logs reference no assets; the flow pack records input logs AGAINST assets, served by the recordInputAgainst write path on the same store — one event kind, two entry points
reversal_condition: a reader rules the second entry point out of contract
